sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to rend, to tear asunder, as garments; for sorrow, Gen 37:29, 34 Gen 37:34 44:13 2Sa 13:31 2Ki 18:37 as a wild beast, the breast of any one, Hos 13:8 God, the heaven, Isa 63:19. קָרַע עֵינַיִם בַּפּוּךְ Jer 4:30, to rend the eyes with paint, by putting too much stibium on them (see כָּחַל , פּוּךְ ).

(2) to rend away, tear out, Lev 13:56 1Sa 15:28, “Jehovah rendeth away the kingdom of Israel from thee;” 1Sa 28:17 1Ki 11:11.

(3) to cut in pieces (with a knife), Jer 36:23.

(4) to cut out (windows in a wall), Jer 22:14.

(5) to tear with words, to curse, Psa 35:15 (Arab. قرع II., to blame, to rebuke. Compare נָקַב No. 1, 2, 3).

Niphal, pass., to be rent; used of garments, Exo 28:32 39:23 of a rent altar, 1Ki 13:3, 1Ki 13:5. Hence-
